classifications.phrase: increase the cash rate target by 50 basis points
committed to returning inflation to the 2–3 per cent range
Inflation in Australia is the highest it has been since the early 1990s
widespread upward pressures on prices from strong demand
tight labour market and capacity constraints in some sectors
Australian economy is continuing to grow solidly
unemployment rate declined further in July to 3.4 per cent
some increase in the unemployment rate is expected as economic growth slows
Wages growth has picked up from the low rates of recent years
further increase in interest rates today will help bring inflation back to target
expects to increase interest rates further over the months ahead
committed to doing what is necessary to ensure that inflation returns to target

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a hawkish stance focused on combating elevated inflation through rate increases, while acknowledging growth and employment strength but noting future moderation.
